title: Implementation constructor does not disable initializers
description: The UUPS implementation `Market` does not call `_disableInitializers()` in its constructor. This lets anyone call `initialize(address)` directly against the implementation address and become the `owner`.
impact: An attacker takes ownership of the implementation contract. Combined with the unprotected `_authorizeUpgrade`, they can upgrade the proxy to arbitrary code and drain any funds routed through it.
recommendation: Add a constructor that calls `_disableInitializers()` on the implementation, per OpenZeppelin's upgradeable-contracts guidance.

title: _authorizeUpgrade has no access control
description: `_authorizeUpgrade(address)` is the UUPS hook that gates upgrades. The override here has an empty body and no modifier, so any address can call `upgradeTo` / `upgradeToAndCall` on the proxy and point it at arbitrary code.
impact: Any EOA can replace the implementation with malicious code and steal any funds or state held by the proxy.
recommendation: Restrict `_authorizeUpgrade` with `onlyOwner` or a dedicated upgrade role, e.g. `function _authorizeUpgrade(address) internal override onlyOwner {}`.
